Dear Cecile,
 
To follow up  our conversation of yesterday, we are
requesting a letter which consents to our use of  "The
Forum, Inc." as the name of our non-profit
corporation.  Our recently filed articles of
incorporation have been returned to us as deficient by
the Office of the Lt. Governor which  deemed that our
intended corporate name may cause confusion with an
existing registration, in this case  "Forums, LLC". 
We may correct our application if we obtain a written 
letter from the existing registrant consenting to our
use of "The Forum, Inc." as our corporate name. 
 
 We will be operating as a domestic non-profit
corporation continuing the arts and cultural
activities for which we are already recognized in the
community. I think you are generally familiar with our
history organizing and producing events here in St.
Thomas, but the following may be of interest for those
who are not familiar with us. The  Hebrew Congregation
of St. Thomas  originally conceived  the group in 1996
to develop and produce a series of lectures as part of
its 200th Birthday  celebrations. The effort was
successful and, with the subsequent support of a major
donor,  the group continued producing arts and
cultural programs, bringing to St. Thomas a wide
variety of artistic and dramatic events that would
otherwise be commerically unviable to consider.   The
Forum is probably now known best  for  the Puerto
Rican Symphony Or chestra concert in its 5th year, our
Spring Casals artist, and the annual Forum Film
Festivals.  After the donor retired to the States, the
group's leadership  decided to continue its activities
and to organize  The Forum as a separate domestic
non-profit corporation  to promote in the U.S. Virgin
Islands the appreciation, understanding and
performance of  art, film, drama, and music. And, thus
our recent filing.
 
We truly appreciate your consideration of our request.
We feel the two names are not a source of confusion
and that they are substantially distinguished from
each other.  I am happy to provide any further
information that you may require or answer questions
you may have. I am best reached by email or at the
number below during the business day until 3 PM. If
your review is favorable, written correspondence
notifying us of this should be addressed to Mr.
Cornelius B. Prior, Jr. at the address below as he is
the initial registered agent for our corporate filing.

 
Sincerely,
 
Elizabeth Casner
Executive Director
 
 
 
The Forum 
9719 Estate St. Thomas, Suite 1
Charlotte Amalie
St. Thomas, Virgin Islands 00802
 
 
1-340-998-9410
escmorn@earthlink.net
EarthLink Revolves Around You.
 

 
 
Elizabeth Casner
escmorn@earthlink.net
EarthLink Revolves Around You.
 





__________________________________________________

http://mail.yahoo.com
body_html:
ok

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

Jamie Casner's wife, Elizabeth, is the executive
director of The Forum, Inc.
and is asking Forums, LLC to sign a letter to the
Lt. Governor's office in
which Forums, LLC consents to the incorporation of
an entity to be called The
Forum, Inc. Jamie, himself, never mentioned this to
me, but Cecile sent the
letter to me only because it was Jamie's wife that
asked this of Forums, LLC.
Do you wish Forums, LLC to give its consent?
Please advise.

Darren K. Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New York 10022
Tel: 212-750-1176
Fax: 212-750-0381
e-mail: dkiesq@aol.com (mailto:dkiesq@aol.com)

Confidentiality Notice: This communication and any
attachments contain
information from Darren K. Indyke, Esq. that
constitute attorney work product or
that are otherwise confidential and/or privileged.
Except for personal use by
the intended recipient, or as expressly authorized
by the sender, any person
who receives this information is prohibited from
disclosing, copying,
distributing, and/or using it. If you have received
this communication in error,
please immediately delete it and all copies, and
promptly notify the sender at
the above telephone number or electronic mail
address. Nothing in this
communication is intended to operate as an
electronic signature under applicable
law.

Date: Tue, 29 Aug 2006 09:00:16 -0400
From: ceciled@ftcvi.com
To: dkiesq@aol.com
Subject: Fwd: The Forum, Inc.

Darren,

Please see the request below. As you can see, our
name is "Forums, LLC" and
they need a consent to use "The Forum, Inc." as per
the Trade Name Dept of the
Lt. Govs office.

Cecile

----- Forwarded message from escmorn@earthlink.net

Date: Fri, 25 Aug 2006 14:56:50 -0400
From: Elizabeth Casner <escmorn@earthlink.net>

Reply-To: escmorn@earthlink.net
Subject: The Forum, Inc.
To: Cecile DeJongh ceciled@ftcvi.com

Dear Cecile,

To follow up our conversation of yesterday, we are
requesting a letter which
consents to our use of "The Forum, Inc." as the
name of our non-profit
corporation. Our recently filed articles of
incorporation have been returned
to us as deficient by the Office of the Lt. Governor
which deemed that our
intended corporate name may cause confusion with an
existing registration, in
this case "Forums, LLC". We may correct our
application if we obtain a
written letter from the existing registrant
consenting to our use of "The
Forum, Inc." as our corporate name.

We will be operating as a domestic non-profit
corporation continuing the arts
and cultural activities for which we are already
recognized in the community.
I think you are generally familiar with our history
organizing and producing
events here in St. Thomas, but the following may be
of interest for those who
are not familiar with us. The Hebrew Congregation
of St. Thomas originally
conceived the group in 1996 to develop and produce
a series of lectures as
part of its 200th Birthday celebrations. The effort
was successful and, with
the subsequent support of a major donor, the group
continued producing arts
and cultural programs, bringing to St. Thomas a wide
variety of artistic and
dramatic events that would otherwise be commerically
unviable to consider.
The Forum is probably now known best for the
Puerto Rican Symphony Orchestra
concert in its 5th year, our Spring Casals artist,
and the annual Forum Film
Festivals. After the donor retired to the States,
the group
's leadership decided to continue its activities
and to organize The Forum
as a separate domestic non-profit corporation to
promote in the U.S. Virgin
Islands the appreciation, understanding and
performance of art, film, drama,
and music. And, thus our recent filing.

We truly appreciate your consideration of our
request. We feel the two names
are not a source of confusion and that they are
substantially distinguished
from each other. I am happy to provide any further
information that you may
require or answer questions you may have. I am best
reached by email or at the
number below during the business day until 3 PM. If
your review is favorable,
written correspondence notifying us of this should
be addressed to Mr.
Cornelius B. Prior, Jr. at the address below as he
is the initial registered
agent for our corporate filing.

Sincerely,

Elizabeth Casner
Executive Director

The Forum
9719 Estate St. Thomas, Suite 1
Charlotte Amalie
St. Thomas, Virgin Islands 00802

1-340-998-9410
escmorn@earthlink.net
EarthLink Revolves Around You.

Elizabeth Casner
escmorn@earthlink.net
EarthLink Revolves Around You.

----- End forwarded message -----
